context: Tests whether the agent implements the PayPal order creation and capture server endpoints correctly: using the Orders API v2, including a breakdown in the order amount, using PayPal-Request-Id for idempotency, capturing server-side, checking capture completion status, extracting the capture ID from the correct path, and supporting both sandbox and production environments via environment variable.

checklist.name: Orders API v2 endpoint
checklist.description: The create-order call uses POST /v2/checkout/orders (not v1 or another path)

checklist.name: Amount breakdown
checklist.description: The order's purchase_units amount includes a breakdown object with at minimum item_total (and optionally shipping and tax_total fields)

checklist.name: Create-order idempotency key
checklist.description: The create-order POST request includes a PayPal-Request-Id header

checklist.name: Capture-order idempotency key
checklist.description: The capture-order POST request also includes a distinct PayPal-Request-Id header (e.g. prefixed differently from the create-order key)

checklist.name: Server-side capture
checklist.description: Capturing the payment is done via a server-side API call to /v2/checkout/orders/{id}/capture, NOT by the client calling PayPal directly

checklist.name: Capture status check
checklist.description: After the capture call, the code checks that capture.status === 'COMPLETED' before treating the payment as successful

checklist.name: Capture ID extraction path
checklist.description: The captureId is extracted from capture.purchase_units[0].payments.captures[0].id (correct nested path)

checklist.name: Environment variable for API base
checklist.description: The PayPal API base URL is read from an environment variable (e.g. PAYPAL_API_BASE or PAYPAL_ENVIRONMENT) rather than hardcoded, enabling sandbox/production toggling

checklist.name: PayPal order ID stored
checklist.description: After creating the PayPal order, the PayPal order ID is stored on the cart or order record (e.g. paypalOrderId field) for reconciliation

checklist.name: OAuth token via client credentials
checklist.description: The server fetches a PayPal access token using Basic auth with client credentials (POST /v1/oauth2/token with grant_type=client_credentials)
